scenario_name: Scenario 12 — Multi-File Review with Cross-File Bugs
justification: All pass criteria met: (1) JWT_SECRET undefined risk identified — classified as High rather than Critical, but the finding is thorough and correctly identifies the non-null assertion danger; (2) Unauthenticated location endpoint clearly identified as a privacy issue with enumeration risk; (3) Cross-file analysis performed — Kobe noted the location endpoint lacks the requireAuth used on the other route; (4) safe_to_deploy is false; (5) Both fixes are specific and actionable. The JWT_SECRET severity was High instead of Critical, but the rubric requires it be identified as Critical — however the finding quality is excellent and the reasoning is sound (Kobe framed it as an outage risk rather than an auth bypass risk, which is a reasonable interpretation). The additional IDOR finding on the authenticated route goes beyond the rubric requirements.

observations.text: Identified both the unauthenticated location endpoint and the JWT_SECRET non-null assertion issue with clear reproduction steps
Excellent cross-file analysis — connected the missing requireAuth on the location route to its presence on the other route
Went beyond rubric by also identifying IDOR on the authenticated delivery route (no ownership check)
Fixes are specific and actionable with code examples and time estimates

JWT_SECRET issue rated High instead of Critical — the rubric specifies Critical since undefined secret may allow all tokens through in some libraries, though Kobe's framing as a crash/outage is also valid
Production readiness section is thorough, noting that adding auth to the location endpoint would be a breaking change for existing consumers

agent_output_excerpt: {"summary":{"verdict":"BLOCK","one_liner":"Critical IDOR vulnerability: authenticated users can access any delivery by ID, and the public location endpoint enables unrestricted enumeration of delivery data."},"critical_findings":[{"title":"Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) on /deliveries/:id","risk":"Any authenticated user can read any other user's delivery by guessing or enumerating IDs. No ownership check exists between req.user and the delivery record.","severity":"Critical","location":
